title: Secure Healthcare Information Exchange Platform ‘KEEP SAFE’
abstract: In recent years, several hospitals have began maintaining electronic medical records (EMRs) for each patient using health information systems. When a patient visits a new hospital for the first time, the hospital's help desk asks them to fill out relevant personal information and verify their identity on the spot. This patient will find that many of the electronic records they have are in many hospitals' health information systems that were previously visited, and each EMR in these hospitals' information systems cannot be accessed or shared between these hospitals. It is inconvenient for this patient because they will have to provide their records again and retell their medical history. In addition to being time consuming, this is not practical. Thus, in this project, we propose a practical and provable patient EMR fair exchange scheme. In this system, medical records are securely stored using blockchain technology, enabling decentralised data protection in healthcare and avoiding specific threats. Patients can securely share their EMRs using barcodes or their National IDs/Residence Numbers. The patient can also control who accesses the EMR. This project aims to facilitate the exchange of medical records in the Saudi Arabian community.

introduction.problem: Today, most Saudi Arabian hospitals have their own applications. Each application offers different functionality. As a result of no communication between hospitals regarding patient information, when a patient transfers from one hospital to another, this process is carried out on paper or through file exchange between patients and their doctors. As a result, there are still some overhead costs on medical staff. They need to enter all patient information which is already done in another hospital. This takes other patients' time and may result in mistakes when entering the information. Medical errors could have serious consequences. Therefore, it is important to ensure that there is a proper system in place that allows for the easy transfer of patient information between hospitals. It will reduce overhead costs, save time, and prevent medical errors. Developing HCIE aligns with the Vision 2030 of Saudi Arabia, which aims to improve health care standards, quality, and accessibility.
introduction.aim: The aim of this graduation project is to develop a healthcare exchange platform that enhances the safe sharing of patient information among healthcare providers, allows patients to keep their health data on hand for easy access, and improves care coordination and reduces cost and errors.
objectives: Provide patients with the ability to share their medical records securely with healthcare providers.

Between different healthcare providers.
Provide the healthcare provider access to the patient medical records.

conclusion.results: the project successfully accomplished some of its objectives. As a first step, we developed an application that allows a patient to create an account on this platform to view and share his medical records. As a second step, we developed a website for healthcare providers to view the medical records shared by their patients and upload new records. In order to make sure that every medical record is valid and secure, we use blockchain technology. We encountered some difficulties with blockchain because it was a new feature and there was some information that was still incomplete. We faced challenges understanding the technology and making accurate predictions about its implications. We also had difficulty finding experienced resources to help us with our project.
conclusion.future_work: Enhancing and expanding our application. This can be achieved by incorporating a range of features to enhance the security and reliability of our system.  Enhancing and expanding your application. Provide the patient with the option of adding a family member.  Making our application available to all Saudi Arabian hospitals and clinics. 104  Enhancing and expanding our application. By making it compatible with iOS.  Enhancing and expanding our application. By adding Arabic language support. Developing our application so that it can be used by all healthcare providers and patients. Improve performance to ensure that the application is always available. Enhancing and expanding our application to be as easy to use as possible even for people who do not know anything about computers. Enhancing and expanding our application. by making the app Ministry of Health certified
